headline: Quota System in Professional Bodies Limits Non-Bumiputera Licences
context: Investigations by the Malaysian Competition Commission found informal quota systems in 4 professional licensing bodies that limit non-Bumiputera licence issuance for architects, contractors, and customs agents.

cards.big: 4 professional bodies operate informal racial quotas on licence issuance.
cards.sub: Non-Bumiputera applicants face 3-5x longer waiting periods despite identical qualifications.

cards.big: Customs agent licence approval: Bumiputera 4 months, non-Bumiputera 18 months.
cards.sub: No published policy justifies the differential. It is administrative practice without legal basis.

cards.big: The Competition Act 2010 prohibits anti-competitive practices.
cards.sub: MyCC's report was submitted to relevant ministries in 2024. No action has been taken.

cards.big: Licence restrictions reduce market competition by an estimated RM2.8B annually.
cards.sub: Consumers pay higher prices when qualified professionals are excluded from the market.

cards.big: Informal quotas in professional licensing are illegal under competition law. They persist because nobody enforces the law against them.

Professional licensing should be based on qualification, not ethnicity. If the Competition Commission identifies illegal quotas, enforcement must follow. Otherwise, the commission is documenting violations, not preventing them.
